tossups.question: The earliest description of this painting is from a sonnet by Bernardo Bellincioni which opens by asking “Why are you angry? Whom do you envy, nature?” Using layer-amplification analysis, Pascal Cotte found that one of this painting’s title figures was altered to be larger, lighter-colored, and more muscular, likely because it referred to a noble’s nickname. A 20th century restoration removed the footprint of an SS soldier from this painting. A 1497 letter requested that this painting be loaned to Isabella d’Este (“DESS-tay”). Because this painting’s background was repainted (*) black in the 18th century and its subject wears a thin, dark chain across the forehead, it resembles the artist’s painting La Belle Ferronnière. The Czartoyski (“char-TOY-skee”) Museum houses this National Treasure of Poland, which depicts Ludovico Sforza’s (“s’FORT-sah’s”) mistress Cecilia Gallerani (“che-CHEE-lee-ah gal-leh-RAH-nee”). For 10 points, name this Leonardo portrait of a woman holding a short-tailed weasel.
tossups.answer: Lady with an Ermine [or Dama con l'ermellino or Dama z gronostajem]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Lady with an Ermine [or Dama con l'ermellino or Dama z gronostajem]
tossups.question_sanitized: The earliest description of this painting is from a sonnet by Bernardo Bellincioni which opens by asking "Why are you angry? Whom do you envy, nature?" Using layer-amplification analysis, Pascal Cotte found that one of this painting's title figures was altered to be larger, lighter-colored, and more muscular, likely because it referred to a noble's nickname. A 20th century restoration removed the footprint of an SS soldier from this painting. A 1497 letter requested that this painting be loaned to Isabella d'Este ("DESS-tay"). Because this painting's background was repainted (*) black in the 18th century and its subject wears a thin, dark chain across the forehead, it resembles the artist's painting La Belle Ferronniere. The Czartoyski ("char-TOY-skee") Museum houses this National Treasure of Poland, which depicts Ludovico Sforza's ("s'FORT-sah's") mistress Cecilia Gallerani ("che-CHEE-lee-ah gal-leh-RAH-nee"). For 10 points, name this Leonardo portrait of a woman holding a short-tailed weasel.

bonuses.leadin: The polished jet necklace of this painting’s subject may be a reference to its patron's nickname of “the Moor” due to his dark complexion. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this portrait of Cecilia Gallerini holding the title white animal that symbolizes her pregnancy with Ludovico Sforza.
Lady with an Ermine is a portrait by this artist, whose only painting on public view in the Americas is his Portrait of Ginevra de’ Benci. Lisa del Giocondo modeled for this artist’s Mona Lisa.

The Czartoryski (zar-tor-EE-skee) family gave Lady with an Ermine its name to differentiate it from a portrait of Francis I’s mistress Lucretia Crivelli wearing this jewelry. These thin Renaissance headbands have a jewel in the center.
answers_sanitized: Leonardo da Vinci [accept either underlined; accept Da Vinky?]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The polished jet necklace of this painting's subject may be a reference to its patron's nickname of "the Moor" due to his dark complexion. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this portrait of Cecilia Gallerini holding the title white animal that symbolizes her pregnancy with Ludovico Sforza.
Lady with an Ermine is a portrait by this artist, whose only painting on public view in the Americas is his Portrait of Ginevra de' Benci. Lisa del Giocondo modeled for this artist's Mona Lisa.

The Czartoryski (zar-tor-EE-skee) family gave Lady with an Ermine its name to differentiate it from a portrait of Francis I's mistress Lucretia Crivelli wearing this jewelry. These thin Renaissance headbands have a jewel in the center.

bonuses.leadin: A recently-uncovered fresco in this ruler's castle shows sixteen mulberry trees rising from the floor towards the ceiling. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this ruler who commissioned the Sala delle Asse (SAH-luh DEL-lay AHS-say). Donato Montorfano's painting of this ruler and his wife standing below a crucifixion hangs opposite the most famous piece that he commissioned for the Santa Maria delle Grazie.
Ludovico Sforza commissioned this artist to paint frescoes in the Sala delle Asse and the now-damaged Last Supper in the Santa Maria delle Grazie.

Da Vinci depicted Cecilia Gallerani, Ludovico's mistress, wearing a blue and red coat while holding the title animal of this painting. This painting was mistakenly given the title of Belle Ferroniere (bel fer-oe-nyear) after it was acquired by a Polish prince.
answers_sanitized: Ludovico Sforza [accept Ludovico "Il Moro"; prompt on Sforza]
Leonardo da Vinci [accept either underlined part]
Lady with an Ermine [or Dama con l'ermellino]

Ludovico Sforza commissioned this artist to paint frescoes in the Sala delle Asse and the now-damaged Last Supper in the Santa Maria delle Grazie.
